content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322-5662 
Gerald A. Sperry 
City Attorney 
City of Stockton 
City Hall 
Stockton, CA 
95202 
Dear Mr. Sperry: 
Administration 
322-5660 
Executive/legal 
32~-5901 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
November 17, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Written 
Advice, Our No. A-83-235 
You have requested our written advice, pursuant to 
Government Code Section 83114(b), on behalf of Stockton City 
Councilmember William Sousa. 
FACTS 
Councilmember Sousa is married and his wife is employed by a 
private hospital and earns salary in excess of $10,000 
annually. 
The hospital has applied to the Stockton City 
Planning Commission for a Use Permit to construct an expansion 
of the hospital's facilities. 
The Planning Commission acted 
favorably on the request, but this has now been appealed to the 
City Council pursuant to the City's ordinances which require the 
Council to schedule and hold a public hearing. 
We may assume 
that the financial effect of this decision on the hospital will 
be foreseeable, material and distinguishable from its effect on 
the public generally. 
QUESTIONS 
You have posed the following two questions: 
Is Councilmember Sousa disqualified from: 
(a) 
Voting to adopt the resolution setting the public 
hearing; and/or 
(b) 
Participating in any capacity in the public 
hearing on the appeal? 


Gerald A. Sperry 
November 17, 1983 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act .. !/ (the "Act") prohibits a public 
official from making, participating in making or using his or 
her official position to influence the making of a government 
decision in which he or she has a financial interest. 
Section 
87100. 
An official has a financial interest in a decision 
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on 
the public generally, on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income, ... aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to, received by or promised to the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is 
made. 
Section 87103. 
added. ) 
{Emphasis 
Section 82030 defines income as including the community 
property interest in the income of a spouse. 
Consequently, 
one-half of Mr. Sousa's wife's income from the hospital is 
attributable to him. 
This makes the hospital a source of income 
of $250 or more to Mr. Sousa during the preceding 12 months. 
Since it is agreed that the decision on the appeal will have a 
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the 
hospital, Mr. Sousa has a financial interest in the decision 
within the meaning of Section 87103 and is required by Section 
87100 to disqualify himself from any form of participation in 
that decision. 
You also have asked whether Mr. Sousa may participate in 
adopting the resolution to set the hearing, an action required 
by the City ordinances. 
So long as the action is purely 
ministerial, it does not constitute a "decision" within the 
meaning of Section 87100. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
II Government Code Sections 81000-91014. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
specified. 


Gerald A. Sperry 
November 17, 1983 
Page 3 
18700 (d) (1). 
However, if the action involves discretionary 
matters,~/ then it would not be purely ministerial and would 
require disqualification. 
I trust that this letter resolves your questions 
satisfactorily. 
Should you require further amplification, 
please call me at (916) 322-5901. 
Sincerely, 
-;;;), 
/~ 
ko~er~'-E. Le~d~gh ( 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
REL:plh 
~/ For instance, if the date of the hearing is subject to 
debate and the proposed dates are advantageous or disadvan-
tageous for one party or the other, this would be a 
discretionary matter. 
You have also raised the issue of the 
resolution to set the hearing being included on the Council's 
consent calendar. 
The vote to approve or disapprove the items 
on the consent calendar will likely involve nonministerial 
actions. 
Consequently, that vote would not be exempt under 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 81700(d) (1) and disqualification would 
be required. 
The same would also be true of any decision by 
councilmembers to place the matter on the consent calendar. 


October 18, 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Conflict of Interest Section 
1100 K Street Building 
Sacramento 
CA 
95814 
CONFLICT OF INTEREST LETTER OPINION 
William Sousa, a member of the City Council for the City of 
Stockton, has requested an opinion from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission as to his participation as a councilmember 
under the following circumstances. 
Councilmember Sousa is married and his wife is employed by a 
private hospital and earns salary in excess of $10,000.00. 
Said hospital applied to and received from the Stockton City 
Planning Commission a Use Permit to expand its facilities. 
The Planning Commission's decision has been appealed to the 
City Council pursuant to the City's ordinances which require 
the Council schedule and hold a public hearing. 
Is Councilmember Sousa disqualified from: 
(a) 
voting to adopt the resolution setting the public hearing; 
and/or 
(b) 
participating ln any capacity in the public hearing on the 
appeal? 


FPPC 
10/18/83 
Page 2 
It is the opinion of this office that: 
(a) 
The setting of the public hearing and ordering the City 
Clerk to give notice thereof is a ministerial act required 
by the City's ordinances and Councilmember Sousa is not 
disqualified from voting to adopt the resolution setting 
said hearing. 
(Said resolution is routinely adopted on 
the "CONSENT" calendar.) 
(b) 
Councilmember Sousa is disqualified from participating in 
the public hearing on the appeal as he has a financial 
interest, his community property interest in his wife's 
income, in excess of $250.00 per year. 
Section 87l03(c) 
and Section 87100, Government Code. 
Your review and advice will be appreciated. 
~~~ 
GERALD A. SPERRY 
CITY ATTORNEY 
GAS: cj 
cc: 
Councilmember William Sousa 


tate of CaJiforIlia 
Fair Political Practices CommissioIl. 
P.o. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Gerald A. Sperry 
City Attorney 
City Hall 
Stockton, CA 
95202 
Dear Mr. Sperry: 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
32:?5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
October 21, 1983 
Re: 
A-83-235 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322.6444 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Fishburn, an attorney 
in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
Verr truly yours, 
. 
."

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember whose spouse earns income from a hospital that has applied for a use permit must disqualify himself from voting on setting the public hearing and/or participating in the hearing on the appeal of the Planning Commission's approval.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Councilmember Sousa may vote on the purely ministerial resolution to set the hearing date, but must disqualify himself from participating in the actual public hearing on the appeal because his community property interest in his spouse's income from the hospital creates a financial interest exceeding $250.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains multiple sections: incoming request dated 10/18/83, acknowledgment dated 10/21/83, and detailed FPPC advice letter dated 11/17/83. Primary response document analyzed. Clear Q/C sections with detailed analysis of Government Code sections 87100 and 87103.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have posed the following two questions: 
Is Councilmember Sousa disqualified from: 
(a) 
Voting to adopt the resolution setting the public 
hearing; and/or 
(b) 
Participating in any capacity in the public 
hearing on the appeal? 


Gerald A. Sperry 
November 17, 1983

CONCLUSION: The setting of the public hearing and ordering the City Clerk to give notice thereof is a ministerial act required by the City's ordinances and Councilmember Sousa is not disqualified from voting to adopt the resolution setting said hearing. Councilmember Sousa is disqualified from participating in the public hearing on the appeal as he has a financial interest, his community property interest in his wife's income, in excess of $250.00 per year.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that a city councilmember whose spouse earns more than $10,000 annually from a hospital seeking a use permit may vote on the ministerial resolution setting a public hearing on appeal, but must disqualify himself from participating in the substantive hearing on the appeal due to his financial interest through community property.
